summary: Tested Keyword Intelligence bug fix end-to-end. Main bug is FIXED: /api/sites/{id}/keyword-intelligence job completes in ~150s with status 'completed', no 'Expecting delimiter' error. Result contains 6 clusters, quick_wins, content_plan, competitors, summary. /latest endpoint also returns full report. However, one edge case in _repair_json fails: the exact truncation case from the review request '{"a": [{"b": "c", "d"' repairs to '{"a": [{"b": "c", "d"}]}' which is still invalid JSON (key without colon/value).

minor.issue: Fails on truncation where a key is present without ':' — e.g. '{"a": [{"b": "c", "d"' produces '{"a": [{"b": "c", "d"}]}' which is still invalid. The regex on line 381 only removes 'key:' patterns with trailing colon; it does not remove a bare trailing '"key"' after a comma. In production this is mitigated by max_tokens=16000 + retry(2), and real e2e job completed successfully, but the robustness helper is not fully correct.

action_items: Improve _repair_json in /app/backend/ai_visibility.py to also strip a trailing bare key without value (e.g., add regex to remove ',\s*"[^"]*"\s*$' after removing 'key:' pattern), so that the exact test case from the bug report parses cleanly. Suggested addition: after line 381 add: s = re.sub(r',\s*"[^"]*"\s*$', '', s).
critical_code_review_comments: _repair_json handles many truncation cases but misses 'key without colon' — should also drop dangling '"key"' fragments after commas.

keyword_intelligence._llm retries with the SAME prompt/session (session_id changes with attempt suffix which is fine); combined with max_tokens=16000 and the repair helper, real-world Claude responses parse successfully.
server.py is very large (>3900 lines) — consider splitting router modules.
seed_data_creation: None (used existing Logirent site b3bec42d-2117-4f73-93b9-a0adc3b47a38 with existing business profile)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full e2e keyword-intelligence job takes ~150s. The main user-reported bug ('Expecting delimiter') is resolved by max_tokens=16000 + retry — real Claude calls succeed. Only remaining issue is a small gap in _repair_json regex that fails on one specific truncation pattern.
rca of the issue: Original bug: Claude JSON response truncated by max_tokens (previously default 4096) → invalid JSON at char 13897 → json.JSONDecodeError. Fix combines: (a) with_params(max_tokens=16000) — main mitigation, (b) _repair_json to close open brackets/strings, (c) retry(2) on parse failure. E2E test confirms the combination works. Residual: _repair_json doesn't handle bare '"key"' after comma without colon — low-priority since retries + higher token budget make it unlikely to trigger.
